notes: The Rausser College of Natural Resources (RCNR) admits students directly into one of its nine undergraduate majors as selected by the applicant at the time of application. Because Rausser College does not admit transfer students as undeclared, each individual major has a distinct set of transfer courses required as major preparation before the applicant is minimally eligible for admission. Resources for RCNR transfer applicants: UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admissions Minimum Requirements for Transfer Admission Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page - includes transfer admission guidelines specific to each Rausser College major, as well as additional information on how to submit a competitive application for your intended major Rausser College Major Requirements and Sample Degree Plans Rausser College Advising Staff

ABOUT THE NUTRITION & METABOLIC BIOLOGY MAJOR The Nutrition & Metabolic Biology major at UC Berkeley provides a strong foundation in the biological and chemical sciences. Students are educated in the experimental biology of metabolic regulation, the impact of genetics on use of dietary constituents, and the interaction among genetics, health/chronic disease and dietary chemicals. This program prepares students to perform research into the relationship between diet and vertebrate development and well-being mechanistically, and/or for health professional careers. For additional information, please contact the Nutritional Sciences advisor at nst.ugrad@berkeley.edu .

Please Note: Applicants are expected to complete all required coursework by the end of the spring term prior to admission. Majors may allow one course pending in the summer term prior to admission if the major has more than five required courses and at least five courses of the required coursework have been completed; in this case, if a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. Majors which require five or fewer required courses to transfer will not allow required coursework to be completed in the summer prior to admission. Please see the Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page for individual requirements by major. Nutrition & Metabolic Biology applicants may have the opportunity to complete a single required course in the summer term prior to admission. If a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. To ensure full articulation of transfer coursework: if a series of courses at a community college is required (e.g., Chemistry 101 + 102 + 103 = CHEM 1A/1AL + CHEM 1B at UC Berkeley), all of the courses in the series must be completed, and must (unless otherwise indicated) be completed at the same community college. Partial completion of the series (e.g., two of the three required courses) may result in no credit toward the requirement(s), and splitting a series of courses between different community colleges is not guaranteed to satisfy the requirement. Courses taken out of series will be evaluated by college faculty during the transfer admissions process. If you have questions about the articulation of a series for your intended major, please email nst.ugrad@berkeley.edu . Minimum Admissions Requirements in Nutrition & Metabolic Biology: Equivalent of: ENGLISH R1A and ENGLISH R1B - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit NUSCTX 10 CHEM 1A and CHEM 1AL - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit CHEM 3A and CHEM 3AL CHEM 3B and CHEM 3BL BIOLOGY 1A and BIOLOGY 1AL MCELLBI 32 and MCELLBI 32L MATH 16A and MATH 16B; or MATH 1A and MATH 1B - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit STAT 2 - may also be satisfied by AP/IB/A-Level exam credit Highly Recommended (but not required) Prior to Transfer PHYSICS 8A - students pursuing a pre-health curriculum may also be required to complete the second half of physics (PHYSICS 8B at Berkeley), which is not required by the Nutrition & Metabolic Biology major 14 units of breadth coursework in the social sciences, humanities, or foreign languages - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit
